channel.title: The Vera Project
channel.description: Upcoming events for The Vera Project

item.title: Veracity Presents: Visitor Coach and more!
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Veracity Presents Visitor Coach, Yellow Peddled Flowers, Silvermoon, Mad King Benefit for Casa Latina \----- Please note that Ticket Blaster shows are free for the community. After the first band starts we will let in folks who may have not reserved a ticket. Please review our FAQs for more information and thank you joining us for this!

Event details

item.title: SP 202: Fabric Printing PILOT *MEMBERS ONLY*
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

This class is only available for VERA members. If you are looking to sign up for SP 202: Fabric Printing, there is another class available for sign up on June 13th! Do you dream of people wearing your artwork out on the streets? Want to make shirts for your friend’s music project? Level up your fabric printing experience and take home your own printed tote bag in this 3-hour intermediate-level fabric printing class! In this hands-on workshop, you’ll explore the use-cases for printing on fabric, practice properly setting up on the t-shirt press, and registering a 2-color fabric print. Students will receive three shirts to print on (sizes S—3XL are available). Students will be printing a Vera specific design, exclusive to the 202 class. Students must complete Screenprint 101 before attending this class.

Event details

item.title: LS 301: Front of House Mixing
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The final course of VERA’s Live Sound series, this three-hour workshop includes practice mixing a live band in a low pressure environment, starting with the basics of soundcheck and ending with a full blown mix. Subject matter will include: Setting up a front of house mix on an M32 board, preamps, EQ settings, mixing styles for different genres, sound pressure levels, outboard gear techniques and lots of critical listening to ensure that show attendees, artists, and staff have a great experience at the show. Students must complete both LS 201: Small PA Mixing and LS 202: Mixing for Monitors before you can take this class. This class is a prerequisite for shadowing and ultimately becoming the Lead Sound Engineer at Vera Project shows. Each Live Sound class unlocks new audio engineering volunteer shifts at Vera and eventually paid opportunities in and outside of the venue. Become a volunteer at Vera to access other audio-related educational programs such as tours, field trips, and internships.

Event details

item.title: SY 101: Basics of Electronic Audio
item.description:
Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Interested in learning more about synthesizers? In this informative 2-hour workshop, our instructor will provide you with a brief history of synthesis and a detailed breakdown of Basic Subtractive Patching! *The use of a laptop and headphones are required to participate in this class. You will need to download the free software VCV Rack. Vera has a limited amount of laptops that we can provide on a first-come-first-serve basis to our members. If you do have your own laptop, we would appreciate if you used your own, so we can provide laptops to those who may not have one. If you would like to request a laptop, please email yewon@theveraproject.org. You will also need to download a couple files for this workshop. These files will be sent 24 hours before the class.

Event details

item.title: LS 101: Basics of Live Sound
item.description:
Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Basics of Live Sound is a 2-hour introduction to live audio engineering and an orientation to Vera Project’s methods and equipment. Topics covered include an introduction to microphones and mic placement, signal paths, stands, cables, DI’s, snake, sub-snakes and etiquette for working with bands. This class is designed for both the novices and experts of all ages and will give an overview of signal flow and setting up small PAs, in addition to the basic requirements for being part of the Vera sound team at shows. Discover why the accommodating, open minded “Vera Attitude” of show production is appreciated worldwide! This class is a prerequisite for advanced audio classes at Vera and Sound Runner shifts at Vera Project show. Each Live Sound class unlocks new audio engineering volunteer shifts at Vera and eventually paid opportunities in and outside of the venue. Become a volunteer at Vera to access other audio-related educational programs such as tours, field trips, and internships!

Event details

item.title: SP 101: Intro to VERA's Screen Print Studio
item.description:
Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

This 2-hour, comprehensive hands-on class teaches you all the basics of screen printing at VERA’s screen printing studio: From hand-drawing a stencil on transparency, coating and burning a screen, to pulling your very first print! Get comfortable with Vera's shop layout, equipment, and culture. Learn techniques for creating screens, terminology, and more to create 1-color projects in the print shop. Students will walk away with the basic skills for screen printing 1-color paper prints, and their own print of a special Vera design, specific to the 101 class. This class is a prerequisite for 200-level classes at VERA, as well as access to Open Sessions to work on your own projects in VERA’s screen printing studio.

Event details

item.title: Claire, Mourn Me, Red Touches Yellow
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Vera Project Presents Claire, Mourn Me, Red Touches Yellow @ Vera Gallery

Event details

item.title: LET IT RIP TOUR
item.description:
Friday, April 17, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Vera Project Presents Adjust the Sails Thoughts on Bowling halftimeshow MooseCreek Park @ Vera Gallery Always All Ages

Event details

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

Event details

item.title: Soul Blind
item.description:
Friday, April 24, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Vera Project Presents Soul Blind w/ Split Chain Downward dream fatigue

Event details

item.title: CL 102: Portable Lighting
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

If you're interested in running and hosting shows in unconventional locations, this is the class for you! In this interactive 2-hour workshop, students will learn the in’s and out’s of installing and programming lights for live events outside of the venue! Participants will explore areas such as installing light trees, cable management, programming lights, light design and placement, and more. This class is great for those who are interested in improving their concert lighting skills, especially for DIY shows or outdoor spaces.

Event details

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

Event details

item.title: RS 101: Intro to Vera's Recording Studio
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Get into VERA’s Recording Studio through this 2-hour workshop and studio orientation! Learn the studio infrastructure including, bootup, signalflow, patchbay setup, mixing desk operation, and basic Digital Audio Workstation (DAW) integration. During this session, participants will experiment with different ways of running signals from the live room to the computer. Some familiarity with Digital Audio Workstations (i.e. ProTools, Logic, Reaper, Ableton, etc.) is encouraged. After taking this course, students who are new to studio recording should be ready for the multi-week Recording Studio Workshop Series, as well as having a better grounding for independent research. Students who are experienced with studio recording may be credentialed for volunteering at studio sessions.

Event details

item.title: Remember Sports
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Vera Project Presents Remember Sports youbet Versing Always All Ages

Event details

item.title: Friend & Gramma
item.description:
Tuesday, April 28, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Vera Project Presents Friend & Gramma w/ guest \----- Please note that Ticket Blaster shows are free for the community. After the first band starts we will let in folks who may have not reserved a ticket. Please review our FAQs for more information and thank you joining us for this!

Event details

item.title: Nuvolascura, Depiction of Man, Crossed, Hetta, Ostraca
item.description:
Friday, May 1, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Vera Project Presents: Nuvolascura (Los Angeles, CA) Crossed (Spain) Hetta (Portugal) Ostraca (Richmond, VA) Depiction of Man

Event details

item.title: CL 101: Intro to Concert Lighting
item.description:
Saturday, May 2, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

In this 2-hour workshop, students will have a practical grasp on lighting concepts and console operation, learn about different lighting instruments, and gain hands-on experience programming lights for Vera’s mainstage. Participants of the class will also gain basic fundamental knowledge of working with artists and production staff to elevate the concert experience as a lighting engineer. Complete this class to be eligible for Lighting Engineer volunteer shifts at Vera shows. Become a volunteer at Vera to access other production-related opportunities!

Event details

item.title: LS 101: Basics of Live Sound
item.description:
Saturday, May 2, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Basics of Live Sound is a 2-hour introduction to live audio engineering and an orientation to Vera Project’s methods and equipment. Topics covered include an introduction to microphones and mic placement, signal paths, stands, cables, DI’s, snake, sub-snakes and etiquette for working with bands. This class is designed for both the novices and experts of all ages and will give an overview of signal flow and setting up small PAs, in addition to the basic requirements for being part of the Vera sound team at shows. Discover why the accommodating, open minded “Vera Attitude” of show production is appreciated worldwide! This class is a prerequisite for advanced audio classes at Vera and Sound Runner shifts at Vera Project show. Each Live Sound class unlocks new audio engineering volunteer shifts at Vera and eventually paid opportunities in and outside of the venue. Become a volunteer at Vera to access other audio-related educational programs such as tours, field trips, and internships!

Event details

item.title: SP 101: Intro to VERA's Screen Print Studio
item.description:
Saturday, May 2, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

This 2-hour, comprehensive hands-on class teaches you all the basics of screen printing at VERA’s screen printing studio: From hand-drawing a stencil on transparency, coating and burning a screen, to pulling your very first print! Get comfortable with Vera's shop layout, equipment, and culture. Learn techniques for creating screens, terminology, and more to create 1-color projects in the print shop. Students will walk away with the basic skills for screen printing 1-color paper prints, and their own print of a special Vera design, specific to the 101 class. This class is a prerequisite for 200-level classes at VERA, as well as access to Open Sessions to work on your own projects in VERA’s screen printing studio.

Event details

item.title: Field Medic
item.description:
Tuesday, May 5, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Vera Project Field Medic \+ Beaming 7 pm doors || 7:30 pm show

Event details

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Wednesday, May 6, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

Event details

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Friday, May 8, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

Event details

item.title: Baby Bugs
item.description:
Friday, May 8, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Vera Project Presents Baby Bugs Elliot Lee

Event details

item.title: SP 201: Poster Printing
item.description:
Saturday, May 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Show posters, art prints, and flat-stock paper print projects galore! In this hands-on workshop, students will build on the basics of paper-based printing by practicing with a two-color design, specifically designed for the Poster Printing class. Learn concepts such as the basics of color separation, print registration, proper equipment use, and helpful tips to successfully run a series of prints in a DIY or professional studio. Pre-requisites: Students must complete Screenprint 101 before attending this class. The Vera Project does not provide refunds nor reschedule tickets for other class dates.

Event details

item.title: LS 201: Small PA Mixing
item.description:
Saturday, May 9, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Small PA Mixing workshop is designed to familiarize students with running a small powered speaker system. Small PAs are generally used for band practices, acoustic sets, DJ sets, poetry readings, protests, or even house shows and shows in Vera’s gallery space!. Topics will include: speaker amplification and setup, practicing cable management and signal flow, using EQ to ‘ring out’ a room, and mixing for small room shows. You must complete LS 101: Basics of Live Sound before you can take this class. This class is a prerequisite for the Front of House Mixing class at Vera Project shows and access to VERA’s PA rental system. Each Live Sound class unlocks new audio engineering volunteer shifts at Vera and eventually paid opportunities in and outside of the venue. Become a volunteer at Vera to access other audio-related educational programs such as tours, field trips, and internships.

Event details

item.title: Ritt Momney
item.description:
Tuesday, May 12, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Vera Project Presents Ritt Momney w/ mercury \------------------- Always All Ages

Event details

item.title: SP 101: Intro to VERA's Screen Print Studio
item.description:
Wednesday, May 13, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

This 2-hour, comprehensive hands-on class teaches you all the basics of screen printing at VERA’s screen printing studio: From hand-drawing a stencil on transparency, coating and burning a screen, to pulling your very first print! Get comfortable with Vera's shop layout, equipment, and culture. Learn techniques for creating screens, terminology, and more to create 1-color projects in the print shop. Students will walk away with the basic skills for screen printing 1-color paper prints, and their own print of a special Vera design, specific to the 101 class. This class is a prerequisite for 200-level classes at VERA, as well as access to Open Sessions to work on your own projects in VERA’s screen printing studio.

Event details

item.title: Dead Calm
item.description:
Saturday, May 16, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Crocodile Presents Dead Calm

Event details

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Tuesday, May 19, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

Basics of Live Sound is a 2-hour introduction to live audio engineering and an orientation to Vera Project’s methods and equipment. Topics covered include an introduction to microphones and mic placement, signal paths, stands, cables, DI’s, snake, sub-snakes and etiquette for working with bands. This class is designed for both the novices and experts of all ages and will give an overview of signal flow and setting up small PAs, in addition to the basic requirements for being part of the Vera sound team at shows. Discover why the accommodating, open minded “Vera Attitude” of show production is appreciated worldwide! This class is a prerequisite for advanced audio classes at Vera and Sound Runner shifts at Vera Project show. Each Live Sound class unlocks new audio engineering volunteer shifts at Vera and eventually paid opportunities in and outside of the venue. This 2-hour, comprehensive hands-on class teaches you all the basics of screen printing at VERA’s screen printing studio: From hand-drawing a stencil on transparency, coating and burning a screen, to pulling your very first print! Get comfortable with Vera's shop layout, equipment, and culture. Learn techniques for creating screens, terminology, and more to create 1-color projects in the print shop. Students will walk away with the basic skills for screen printing 1-color paper prints, and their own print of a special Vera design, specific to the 101 class. Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Vera Project Presents Prince Daddy and Heyna Remo Drive Restraining Order Always All Ages

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Saturday, May 30, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

Event details

item.title: DJ 101: Intro to Digital Dejaying
item.description:
Saturday, May 30, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Interested in learning more about DJ-ing? In this 3-hour crash course, students will learn the ins-and-outs of Serato, playing and mixing tracks, finessing your tracks, and general tips and tricks for digital Dejaying! *Use of a laptop is required to take part in this class — Vera has a limited number of laptops available to borrow for membership. If you need one to participate, please email yewon.k@theveraproject.org to confirm our availability.

Event details

item.title: RS 101: Intro to Vera's Recording Studio
item.description:
Saturday, May 30, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Get into VERA’s Recording Studio through this 2-hour workshop and studio orientation! Learn the studio infrastructure including, bootup, signalflow, patchbay setup, mixing desk operation, and basic Digital Audio Workstation (DAW) integration. During this session, participants will experiment with different ways of running signals from the live room to the computer. Some familiarity with Digital Audio Workstations (i.e. ProTools, Logic, Reaper, Ableton, etc.) is encouraged. After taking this course, students who are new to studio recording should be ready for the multi-week Recording Studio Workshop Series, as well as having a better grounding for independent research. Students who are experienced with studio recording may be credentialed for volunteering at studio sessions.

Event details

item.title: Daisy Grenade
item.description:
Saturday, May 30, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Vera Project Presents Daisy Grenade Vienna Vienna \------------------------ $1 from every ticket purchased will be donated to Let’s Give A Damn

Event details

item.title: True North & Out In Front: I Dragged Us In Another Life Tour 2026
item.description:
Tuesday, June 2, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Vera Project Presents True North & Out In Front I Dragged Us In Another Life Tour 2026 w/ PINKNOISE

Event details

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Wednesday, June 3, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

Event details

item.title: Seahaven
item.description:
Friday, June 5, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Vera Project Presents Seahaven w/ Breakup Shoes & House and Home

Event details

item.title: CL 101: Intro to Concert Lighting
item.description:
Saturday, June 6, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

In this 2-hour workshop, students will have a practical grasp on lighting concepts and console operation, learn about different lighting instruments, and gain hands-on experience programming lights for Vera’s mainstage. Participants of the class will also gain basic fundamental knowledge of working with artists and production staff to elevate the concert experience as a lighting engineer. Complete this class to be eligible for Lighting Engineer volunteer shifts at Vera shows. Become a volunteer at Vera to access other production-related opportunities!

Event details

item.title: LS 101: Basics of Live Sound
item.description:
Saturday, June 6, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Basics of Live Sound is a 2-hour introduction to live audio engineering and an orientation to Vera Project’s methods and equipment. Topics covered include an introduction to microphones and mic placement, signal paths, stands, cables, DI’s, snake, sub-snakes and etiquette for working with bands. This class is designed for both the novices and experts of all ages and will give an overview of signal flow and setting up small PAs, in addition to the basic requirements for being part of the Vera sound team at shows. Discover why the accommodating, open minded “Vera Attitude” of show production is appreciated worldwide! This class is a prerequisite for advanced audio classes at Vera and Sound Runner shifts at Vera Project show. Each Live Sound class unlocks new audio engineering volunteer shifts at Vera and eventually paid opportunities in and outside of the venue. Become a volunteer at Vera to access other audio-related educational programs such as tours, field trips, and internships!

Event details

item.title: SP 101: Intro to VERA's Screen Print Studio
item.description:
Saturday, June 6, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

This 2-hour, comprehensive hands-on class teaches you all the basics of screen printing at VERA’s screen printing studio: From hand-drawing a stencil on transparency, coating and burning a screen, to pulling your very first print! Get comfortable with Vera's shop layout, equipment, and culture. Learn techniques for creating screens, terminology, and more to create 1-color projects in the print shop. Students will walk away with the basic skills for screen printing 1-color paper prints, and their own print of a special Vera design, specific to the 101 class. This class is a prerequisite for 200-level classes at VERA, as well as access to Open Sessions to work on your own projects in VERA’s screen printing studio.

Event details

item.title: SP 101: Intro to VERA's Screen Print Studio
item.description:
Wednesday, June 10, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

This 2-hour, comprehensive hands-on class teaches you all the basics of screen printing at VERA’s screen printing studio: From hand-drawing a stencil on transparency, coating and burning a screen, to pulling your very first print! Get comfortable with Vera's shop layout, equipment, and culture. Learn techniques for creating screens, terminology, and more to create 1-color projects in the print shop. Students will walk away with the basic skills for screen printing 1-color paper prints, and their own print of a special Vera design, specific to the 101 class. This class is a prerequisite for 200-level classes at VERA, as well as access to Open Sessions to work on your own projects in VERA’s screen printing studio.

Event details

item.title: Penelope Trappes
item.description:
Thursday, June 11, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Vera Project Presents Penelope Trappes w/ guest @ Vera Gallery

Event details

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Friday, June 12, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

Event details

item.title: SP 202: Fabric Printing
item.description:
Saturday, June 13, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Do you dream of people wearing your artwork out on the streets? Want to make shirts for your friend’s music project? Level up your fabric printing experience and take home your own printed tote bag in this 3-hour intermediate-level fabric printing class! In this hands-on workshop, you’ll explore the use-cases for printing on fabric, practice properly setting up on the t-shirt press, and registering a 2-color fabric print. Students will receive three shirts to print on (sizes S—3XL are available). Students will be printing a Vera specific design, exclusive to the 202 class. Students must complete Screenprint 101 before attending this class.

Event details

item.title: LS 202: Mixing for Monitors
item.description:
Saturday, June 13, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Creating a good monitor mix is one of the most important tasks for a live sound engineer to achieve. This advanced class will introduce you to digital mixing boards in a low pressure environment. Subjects will include an introduction to Vera’s stage monitors, the basic ‘ringing out’ of monitors, EQing, and setting levels. The use of these techniques will allow you to create a clear monitor mix that will help performers be at their best. After a person has completed the Mixing for Monitors class they are eligible to take on Monitor Engineer volunteer positions at Vera shows. Students must complete Live Sound 101 to be eligible for this class. This class is a prerequisite for the Front of House Mixing class at Vera and Monitor Engineer shifts at Vera Project shows. Each Live Sound class unlocks new audio engineering volunteer shifts at Vera and eventually paid opportunities in and outside of the venue. Become a volunteer at Vera to access other audio-related educational programs such as tours, field trips, and internships.

Event details

item.title: CAMP BLU / FAMOUS FRIEND / FLOATS
item.description:
Saturday, June 13, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Crocodile Presents: CAMP BLU / FAMOUS FRIEND / FLOATS

Event details

item.title: Moon Walker's Wasteland Country Tour
item.description:
Sunday, June 14, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Vera Project Presents Moon Walker w/ Pretoria & Super Cassette Always All Ages

Event details

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Tuesday, June 16, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

Event details

item.title: SY 101: Basics of Electronic Audio
item.description:
Wednesday, June 17, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Interested in learning more about synthesizers? In this informative 2-hour workshop, our instructor will provide you with a brief history of synthesis and a detailed breakdown of Basic Subtractive Patching! *The use of a laptop and headphones are required to participate in this class. You will need to download the free software VCV Rack. Vera has a limited amount of laptops that we can provide on a first-come-first-serve basis to our members. If you do have your own laptop, we would appreciate if you used your own, so we can provide laptops to those who may not have one. If you would like to request a laptop, please email yewon@theveraproject.org. You will also need to download a couple files for this workshop. These files will be sent 24 hours before the class.

Event details

item.title: LS 101: Basics of Live Sound
item.description:
Wednesday, June 17, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Basics of Live Sound is a 2-hour introduction to live audio engineering and an orientation to Vera Project’s methods and equipment. Topics covered include an introduction to microphones and mic placement, signal paths, stands, cables, DI’s, snake, sub-snakes and etiquette for working with bands. This class is designed for both the novices and experts of all ages and will give an overview of signal flow and setting up small PAs, in addition to the basic requirements for being part of the Vera sound team at shows. Discover why the accommodating, open minded “Vera Attitude” of show production is appreciated worldwide! This class is a prerequisite for advanced audio classes at Vera and Sound Runner shifts at Vera Project show. Each Live Sound class unlocks new audio engineering volunteer shifts at Vera and eventually paid opportunities in and outside of the venue. Become a volunteer at Vera to access other audio-related educational programs such as tours, field trips, and internships!

Event details

item.title: SP 101: Intro to VERA's Screen Print Studio
item.description:
Wednesday, June 17, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

This 2-hour, comprehensive hands-on class teaches you all the basics of screen printing at VERA’s screen printing studio: From hand-drawing a stencil on transparency, coating and burning a screen, to pulling your very first print! Get comfortable with Vera's shop layout, equipment, and culture. Learn techniques for creating screens, terminology, and more to create 1-color projects in the print shop. Students will walk away with the basic skills for screen printing 1-color paper prints, and their own print of a special Vera design, specific to the 101 class. This class is a prerequisite for 200-level classes at VERA, as well as access to Open Sessions to work on your own projects in VERA’s screen printing studio.

Event details

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Saturday, June 27, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

item.title: RS 101: Intro to Vera's Recording Studio
item.description:
Saturday, June 27, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Get into VERA’s Recording Studio through this 2-hour workshop and studio orientation! Learn the studio infrastructure including, bootup, signalflow, patchbay setup, mixing desk operation, and basic Digital Audio Workstation (DAW) integration. During this session, participants will experiment with different ways of running signals from the live room to the computer. Some familiarity with Digital Audio Workstations (i.e. ProTools, Logic, Reaper, Ableton, etc.) is encouraged. After taking this course, students who are new to studio recording should be ready for the multi-week Recording Studio Workshop Series, as well as having a better grounding for independent research. Students who are experienced with studio recording may be credentialed for volunteering at studio sessions.
